source.section: Autopilot > AFCS (Automatic Flight Control System) > ATTH/BALT/HSEL/RALT Mode Procedure Example

steps.action: Make sure your stick is centered, no force is applied on it and that you have an appropriate deadzone (see CONTROLS SETUP - WHAT YOU NEED MAPPED section).
steps.voiced: Center the stick with no force applied and verify your deadzone is set.

steps.action: If you intend to use the HSEL (Heading Select) mode, set the desired heading with the HDG switch above the AMPCD screen. Otherwise, disregard this step.
steps.voiced: If using HSEL, set the desired heading with the HDG switch above the AMPCD.

steps.action: Press the A/P button on the UFC (Up-Front Controller) to display autopilot modes
steps.voiced: Press the A/P button on the UFC to display autopilot modes.

steps.action: Press on the OSB (Option Select Button) next to the desired autopilot mode: ATTH: Attitude Hold. Aircraft will maintain the existing pitch and roll attitude between +/- 45 degrees in pitch and +/- 70 degrees in roll BALT: Barometric Altitude Hold. When engaged, aircraft will maintain current heading and barometric altitude between 0 and 70000 ft HSEL: Heading Select. Aircraft will turn to and fly the heading as set on the HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator). RALT: Radar Altitude Hold. Aircraft will maintain current heading and radar altitude between 0 and 5000 ft.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to the desired mode — ATTH holds attitude, BALT holds barometric altitude, HSEL flies the HSI heading, RALT holds radar altitude below 5000 ft.

steps.action: You can disengage autopilot by pressing the Paddle Switch on the control stick.
steps.voiced: Press the Paddle Switch on the control stick to disengage autopilot.
